title: 💻 Claude Code Best Practices: Planning, Context Transfer, ...
content:
Claude Code 技术干货

# Claude Code Best Practices: Planning, Context Transfer, TDD. Use plan mode, CLAUDE.md files, and test-driven development to make the most of Claude's context window. ### Using Claude Code plan mode. Each red-to-green cycle gives Claude unambiguous feedback, and it can iterate through the entire suite without human intervention, making test-driven development (TDD) the single strongest pattern for working with agentic coding tools. **The highest-impact practices are planning before implementation (annotation cycles or plan mode), keeping your `CLAUDE.md` under 150 instructions with progressive disclosure, managing context by clearing sessions at 60% capacity, using hooks for deterministic safety enforcement, and writing tests before implementation so Claude has an external oracle to verify against.**. Learn how to use Claude Code’s Plan Mode to explore your codebase in read-only mode, surface questions, and review structured plans for multi-file changes. Learn how hook-based automation works and get started using Claude Code hooks to automate coding tasks like testing, formatting, and receiving notifications.

title: 💻 What Is Claude Code /simplify and /batch? The Built-In Commands That Fix Overengineering | MindStudio
content:
Claude Code 技术干货

Claude Code's /simplify and /batch commands help you clean up bloated code and run large refactors in parallel. Without `/batch`, you’d need to run Claude Code on each file manually, wait for each operation to complete, and manage the results yourself. When you invoke `/batch`, Claude Code breaks your task into discrete units — one per file, or one per defined scope — and runs them concurrently using multiple Claude instances. 1. **Use `/batch` to identify candidates.** Run a batch scan across your codebase asking Claude Code to flag files with complexity signals — deeply nested functions, functions over a certain length, classes with too many dependencies. Another useful pattern: run `/simplify` on a file, review the output, decide which changes to accept, then use `/batch` to apply those same changes across similar files elsewhere in the codebase. * **`/batch`** runs Claude Code operations in parallel across multiple files — best for large migrations, pattern enforcement, and any change that needs to apply consistently at scale.

title: 💻 Claude Code Batch Processing Complete Guide: /batch Command and Headless Mode in Practice - SmartScope
content:
Claude Code 技术干货

# Claude Code Batch Processing Complete Guide: /batch Command and Headless Mode in Practice¶. The `/batch` command in Claude Code shines when you need to apply the same transformation across hundreds of files. | Aspect | /batch Command | Headless Mode (claude -p) |. `/batch` is a slash command executed within an interactive Claude Code session1. ## Headless Mode (claude -p): CI/CD and Script Integration¶. claude -p "Summarize this file" --output-format text. claude -p "Process this large dataset" --output-format stream-json`. claude -p "Analyze this code" --output-format json | jq -r '.result'. claude -p "Summarize this file" --output-format json | jq '.usage'`. session_id=$(claude -p "Start review" --output-format json \. claude -p "Continue the review" --resume "$session_id"`. claude -p "Review the code" \. - claude -p "Analyze maintainability issues" --output-format json. - claude -p "Check for performance bottlenecks" --output-format json. Claude Code's batch processing is built on two pillars: `/batch` and headless mode (`claude -p`). Claude Code v2.1.63 Release Notes — `/batch` and `/simplify` were introduced in this version.

title: 💻 Claude Code /simplify and /batch Commands Guide
content:
Claude Code 技术干货

# Claude Code /simplify and /batch: Bundled Commands That Ship Code Faster. Claude Code /simplify runs 3-agent code review on your changes. Boris Cherny, the Claude Code PM, put it well: these bundled commands "automate much of the work it used to take to shepherd a pull request to production." `/simplify` is the automated code review step between "it works" and "it's ready to merge.". Claude Code's `/batch` command orchestrates large-scale changes across your codebase in parallel. Each `/batch` worker automatically runs `/simplify` on its own changes before committing. `/simplify` and `/batch` are bundled Claude Code commands -- they ship with every installation and work out of the box. `/simplify` and `/batch` shipped in Claude Code v2.1.63. `/simplify` and `/batch` signal where Claude Code is headed: bundled multi-agent workflows that handle common engineering patterns out of the box. If you want a development framework with pre-built agent orchestration, custom slash commands, and multi-agent patterns that work alongside these bundled commands, the ClaudeFast Code Kit ships with 18 specialized agents and a pipeline designed for exactly this kind of workflow.
